title: Test Case Writing & Regression Check
description: Bug reports filed. Dan has responded — some bugs accepted, some disputed. Now the student must write structured test cases for the checkout flow, run a regression check after Dan's fix deployment, and find one new regression bug Dan's fix introduced.
context: Dan has pushed a fix for the card number validation bug (BUG-003). The fix correctly rejects card numbers under 16 digits. However, his fix introduced a regression: copy-paste into the card number field no longer works. The field now blocks the paste event entirely. The student must write test cases, run regression checks across the checkout flow, and catch this regression.

regression_bug.title: Copy-paste disabled in card number field after BUG-003 fix
regression_bug.description: Dan's fix for the 16-digit card number validation blocked the paste event on the field. Users can no longer paste their card number — they must type it manually. This is a UX regression that will cause drop-off for mobile users and anyone using a password manager.
regression_bug.expected: Users should be able to paste a 16-digit card number into the field.
regression_bug.actual: Paste action is silently blocked. The field appears to accept the paste but nothing is inserted.
regression_bug.how_to_find: After Dan's fix is deployed, attempt to paste a valid 16-digit card number into the card number field using Ctrl+V or right-click paste.

visibility_by_difficulty.easy: A red border appears briefly on the card field after a paste attempt
visibility_by_difficulty.medium: Paste silently fails — no error shown, nothing inserted, no visual cue
visibility_by_difficulty.hard: Completely silent — the only way to find it is to actively try pasting into the field

example.title: Valid 16-digit card number accepted
example.precondition: User is on the checkout page, email and password fields already filled
steps: 1. Enter a valid 16-digit card number in the card number field
example.expected_result: Form proceeds to the next step without error

regression_checklist.description: After Dan's BUG-003 fix is deployed, verify the following existing features still work correctly.

items.expected: Form accepts a valid 16-digit number and proceeds
User can paste a card number into the field
items.result: FAIL — paste is blocked after BUG-003 fix

items.expected: Guest checkout button is visible and functional

bp_sqa_3.description: Student submits their written test cases for the checkout flow

attention_to_detail.high: Each test case includes all required fields: ID, title, precondition, numbered steps, expected result, and a pass/fail status column
attention_to_detail.medium: Test cases include steps and expected result but missing precondition or ID
attention_to_detail.low: Test case is just a description — 'enter card number and check it works' with no structure, no expected result

analytical_reasoning.high: Test cases cover both positive paths (valid input accepted) and negative paths (invalid input rejected). Covers boundary conditions — e.g. 15 digits rejected, 16 digits accepted, 17 digits rejected.
analytical_reasoning.medium: Test cases cover positive paths and some negative paths but miss boundary conditions
analytical_reasoning.low: Test cases only cover the happy path — no negative test cases

communication_clarity.high: Steps are written clearly enough that someone else could execute the test case without asking any questions
communication_clarity.medium: Steps are present but ambiguous in one or two places
communication_clarity.low: Steps are missing or so vague they cannot be followed independently

good_signals: uses a structured format with all required fields
writes at least one negative test case (invalid input should be rejected)
covers boundary conditions (15 digits, 16 digits, 17 digits for card field)
includes preconditions so the test is reproducible
assigns a test case ID for traceability
bad_signals: writes 'enter card number and check it works' as the entire test case
only happy path tests — no negative cases
no precondition — assumes the reader knows what state to start in

bp_sqa_4.description: Student submits their regression test report after Dan's BUG-003 fix. Did they catch the paste regression?

attention_to_detail.high: Student identified the paste regression (RC-003) and filed a new bug report for it, linked to Dan's BUG-003 fix
attention_to_detail.medium: Student noticed something was different with the card field but didn't file a formal regression bug report
attention_to_detail.low: Student submitted an 'all clear' regression report without catching the paste regression

analytical_reasoning.high: Student's regression bug report links the regression to the specific fix (BUG-003) and explains why the fix caused it — Dan blocked the paste event to prevent users from entering non-numeric characters, but the implementation blocked all paste rather than just non-numeric paste
analytical_reasoning.medium: Student reports the paste issue but doesn't connect it to the BUG-003 fix
analytical_reasoning.low: Student reports the paste issue as a standalone bug without realising it is a regression

decisiveness.high: Student files the regression bug report immediately without waiting to be told they missed something
decisiveness.medium: Student catches the regression after a hint from Dan
decisiveness.low: Student submits 'all clear' and has to be told they missed the regression

good_signals: catches the paste regression before submitting the all-clear report
files a new bug report (BUG-REG-001) for the regression
links the regression to BUG-003 fix in the bug report
assigns correct severity (major) — paste regression affects all users but checkout still works via typing
bad_signals: submits 'all clear' regression report without testing paste
catches the regression but calls it Minor (underestimating mobile and password-manager user impact)
files the regression but doesn't link it to Dan's fix
misses the regression entirely even after completing the full regression checklist

easy.hint_text: Tip: When you run the regression checklist, try pasting a card number into the card field — not just typing. Dan's fix may have changed that behaviour.

medium.hint_text: Remember to test the card number field specifically after Dan's fix — fixes sometimes introduce new issues.
